sourceMovie.title: What We Do in the Shadows
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: What We Do in the Shadows (2014) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Taika Waititi's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shaun of the Dead
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If What We Do in the Shadows clicked for you, Shaun of the Dead (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after What We Do in the Shadows?
faq.answer: Try Shaun of the Dead, An American Werewolf in London, The Return of the Living Dead—each shares DNA with What We Do in the Shadows while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is What We Do in the Shadows?
faq.answer: What We Do in the Shadows (2014) is best described as Comedy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is What We Do in the Shadows worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
